In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大すこうしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}} Sugo 178-1 |位置={{Coord|34|31|38|N|135|33|42|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|WD=Q2507|1=April 5}} / {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sugo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Sugo Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大すこうしんしや]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大すこうしんしや]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja}} [[JA:菅生神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619344}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Sugo Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sugo-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}} Sugo 178-1 |位置={{Coord|34|31|38|N|135|33|42|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|WD=Q2507|1=April 5}} / {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sugo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Sugo Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大すこうしんしや]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:美原区の歴史]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja}} [[JA:菅生神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619344}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Sugo Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sugo-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}} Sugo 178-1 |位置={{Coord|34|31|38|N|135|33|42|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|WD=Q2507|1=April 5}} / {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sugo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Sugo Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Mihara Ward]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja}} [[JA:菅生神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619344}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Sugo Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sugo-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}} Sugo 178-1 |位置={{Coord|34|31|38|N|135|33|42|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|WD=Q2507|1=April 5}} / {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sugo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Sugo Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Mihara Ward]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]]

In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Mihara Ward]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sugo-jinja}} [[JA:菅生神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11619344}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Sugo Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sugo-jinja2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}} Sugo 178-1 |位置={{Coord|34|31|38|N|135|33|42|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿=One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|WD=Q2507|1=April 5}} / {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sugo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Sugo Shrine''' (すごうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Mihara-ku|Q1042511|WD=Q1042511|1=Mihara-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Sugo Tenmangū'''. ===Enshrined deities=== Its main deities are {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In addition, due to {{ill|lt=shrine mergers|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} are also enshrined. ===History=== The origin of its foundation is unknown. Many people of {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}} origin lived in this area, came to call themselves the Sugo clan from the place name, and ruled the region as local magnates. It is considered that this Sugo clan enshrined their {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto as their {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in ''Shinshō Kakuchokufu'', which says: “In {{ill|lt=Empress Kōken|Q232955|WD=Q232955|1=Empress Kōken}}, {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|WD=Q30532|1=759}}), one household of provincial fief was allotted.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Sugo Shrine, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. In the medieval period, when {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, shrine monks of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (Kōmatsu-zan Tenmon-ji) promoted a tradition that “Sugakō was born at the edge of Sugasawa in the precincts,” invited Tenjin, and enshrined him together. Sugasawa still exists in the precincts today. In time it came to be called “Sugo Tenmangū,” and in the Edo period Tenjin came to be treated as the main deity. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Related literature=== * Ōue Architectural Research Institute (ed.), ''Report on Repair Work for the Main Sanctuary of Sugo Shrine, Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property'', Sugo Shrine, 2008. * ''Great Heisei Shrine Rebuilding: 350th Anniversary Project for Main Sanctuary Construction: Engishiki Shrine Sugo Tenmangū'', Sugo Tenmangū Heisei Shrine Construction Support Association, 2008. * Mihara History Museum, Sakai City (ed.), ''Cultural Properties of Sugo Shrine: FY2008 Winter Mini-Exhibition'', Mihara History Museum, Sakai City, 2009. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Sugo-jinja}} * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sugojinja.html Sakai City Designated Tangible Cultural Property: Sugo Shrine Main Sanctuary] (Sakai City) * [http://sakai-danjiri.com/ Sakai Danjiri] {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:美原区の建築物]] [[Category:History of Mihara Ward]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Ōsaka Prefecture]]
